You know... i didn't know that this will be this fucking hard.
Look, I'm here. I don't promote myself. But I can say that I more accurate than any of them. Just see my last year performance/analysis on twitter... and see here what I'm doing on Substack.
I would like to show it as many plp as I can, but they just don't listen... It is so tough to deal with it, when you know, you see, and you can't tell it them. 

And plp keep coming with false explainations while your predictions keep being fullfilled day by day, and they don't react. It is not a good feeling.

I guess you all had dreams where you could not yell no matter how hard you tried. Well, this is how it feels.
They are following fake, shilling and lying furus. And i'd like to help, and they still appriciate the non-sense...

comment.body: So I touched on this in a previous daily chat, about China and how they run factories with no AC and the lights off because it is so automated. Given everything you wrote about the labor market and how that isn't a concern for a deflationary spiral, how does that stack up with the fact that, when we onshore again, it will most likely be highly automated and produce less jobs than expected? Could the market also be undervaluing the dramatic effect AI will have on job displacement?

comment.body:
If we get a minor consolidation/relief rally, that'll be your opportunity to reload short.  Folks shouldn't chase this as the risk vs reward isn't there right now.  

If we don't get an opportunity to reload short, sit out and wait for a bottom to flip long.  Bear market rallies are the most vicious rallies
